rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for interface-level assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use visible identifiers from the AADL model to ensure type and scope compatibility.
generic_rules: Component type contracts should define assumptions and guarantees using visible ports and constants.
Implementation-level AGREE clauses must reference valid subcomponents and connections.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers not present in the input AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference component ports in valid scopes.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-specific bindings in component type annexes.
Do not introduce non-visible or package-qualified identifiers in AGREE annexes.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax and annex placement rules
